title: Today's AI Brief: Google's Gemini Spark and Meta's AI Pendant
summary: A look at Google's 24/7 AI assistant Gemini Spark and Meta's reported AI pendant development.
imageAlt: Image from source article for I put Google’s 24/7 AI assistant Gemini Spark to work, and it’s actually pretty useful
imageCaption: I put Google’s 24/7 AI assistant Gemini Spark to work, and it’s actually pretty useful
imageSourceTitle: I put Google’s 24/7 AI assistant Gemini Spark to work, and it’s actually pretty useful

body.text: I'm Leo Stone, and welcome to your AI Brief. Today, I'm looking at a couple of interesting developments in the world of artificial intelligence. First up, I've been checking out Google's new 24/7 AI assistant, Gemini Spark. From what I've seen, it's designed to help you navigate your digital life - essentially, getting your online tasks done and summarizing things you don't have time to read.

According to TechCrunch, Gemini Spark is Google's attempt at creating an agentic assistant. What stands out to me is that it's available 24/7, which could be a game-changer for those of us who can't seem to escape our digital lives. I've been putting it through its paces, and it's actually pretty useful - it can help with tasks and summarize long pieces of text. For instance, I've used it to summarize articles and get a quick rundown of the key points, which has been super helpful.

Also on the board, Meta is reportedly developing an AI-powered pendant. Now, I know what you're thinking - an AI pendant? That sounds like something out of a sci-fi movie. But apparently, Meta is serious about creating a wearable AI device that can assist you throughout the day. I'm curious to see how this one plays out - I mean, who wouldn't want a personal AI assistant hanging around their neck?

My quick read: the artificial intelligence story keeps getting bigger, but also stranger in very human ways. One moment it is strategy and infrastructure; the next, the Vatican is in the room. That is a pretty good sign this technology has left the lab and wandered into the town square.
